summary.intent: Build a React Native Expo web app + Express/WebSocket API server for the Gossamer project to display Claude Code sessions from SQLite in real-time, then progressively improve the UI with table alignment, session linking, scrolling, event rendering, and a tree view.
summary.outcome: Full stack implemented and working: API server with HTTP routes and WebSocket push, Expo web app with ActiveSessions table, SessionDetail with collapsing tool groups, bidirectional plan→implementation session linking, tree view screen, and markdown rendering for important events. All major bugs resolved and running at localhost:19006.

repo: Gossamer uses Prisma 7 + better-sqlite3 for SQLite; db is at ~/.claude/hook-handler.db
When plan mode is approved, Claude Code fires SessionEnd + SessionStart but never fires UserPromptSubmit for the triggering message — the initial prompt is unrecoverable
Plan→implementation session linking: detect SessionStart within 30s of SessionEnd with matching cwd, then set parentSessionId via state.agentParents (same mechanism used for subagents)
NodeNext module resolution requires .js extensions on all local imports in src/server.ts
Dynamic import of server.ts in the serve subcommand prevents express/ws from loading on every hook invocation
childSessionIds are derived at query time from parentSessionId — no schema changes needed; GET /api/sessions builds a childMap in one in-memory pass
SubagentStart hook events were not present in the DB during this session; subagent parent links only come from that hook or the 30s heuristic
updatedAt on some sessions was incorrectly backfilled to the last DB event time rather than the actual session end, causing false positives in the time-overlap heuristic

code.finding: Two-pass groupEvents(): first pass pairs PreToolUse+PostToolUse by tool_use_id into toolUse items; second pass collapses consecutive toolUse and Notification items into a single toolGroup entry. Notifications between tool calls are silently absorbed.

@react-navigation/stack renders screens in absolute-positioned divs on web so flex:1 resolves to 0 height. Fix: useWindowDimensions().height - useHeaderHeight() gives explicit pixel height; combined with overflow:'scroll' on a plain View this creates a proper scrollable viewport.

session?.childSessionIds.map(...) crashes when childSessionIds is undefined (stale API response). Must be session?.childSessionIds?.map(...) — optional chaining must guard the field itself, not just the parent object.

WebSocketServer({ server: httpServer }) shares port 3000 with Express — no separate WS port needed. Polls db.event.findFirst({ orderBy: { id: 'desc' } }) every 2s; broadcasts sessions_updated when max id changes.
Exporting COL_WIDTHS from SessionRow and importing them into the header in ActiveSessions.tsx is the correct pattern for aligning fixed-width table columns across header and row components.

workflow: npm hoisting conflict: parent node_modules/react bleeds into app/ subdirectory; use --legacy-peer-deps or ensure app/ has its own node_modules fully installed
react@18.3.2 does not exist on npm; Expo 52 requires react@18.3.1 exactly
@expo/metro-runtime must be installed explicitly for Expo web support (npx expo install @expo/metro-runtime)
After rebuilding backend (npm run build), the API server process must be restarted manually — the new dist/server.js is not hot-reloaded
Running reconstruct after adding new linking logic in handler.ts backfills parentSessionId for historical sessions without re-inserting events

friction: react@18.3.2 specified in plan does not exist on npm — silent failure during npm install, required manual correction to 18.3.1
Missing @expo/metro-runtime not caught until runtime; Expo web silently fails without it
Scrolling broken across three separate attempts before root cause was identified: @react-navigation/stack absolute-positioned containers make flex:1 resolve to 0 height
Old API server process running stale build caused childSessionIds crash — no obvious indication to the user that the server needed restart
Optional chaining bug (session?.childSessionIds.map vs session?.childSessionIds?.map) caused a hard crash that required context-summary analysis to identify
open_items: SubagentStart hook may not be configured in settings.json — subagent-triggered sessions won't be auto-linked until that hook fires
Time-overlap heuristic for session linking can produce false positives when updatedAt is incorrectly backfilled; needs a more robust end-time source
Tree view uses indented flat-list rendering; could be upgraded to a proper node-edge graph layout (D3-force or react-vis-network) for dense session graphs
Orphaned Notification events that appear in isolation (not adjacent to tool uses) are silently dropped — may hide meaningful permission prompts
